title: Does Blueprinty's Software Actually Help Firms Win More Patents?
summary: Building a Poisson regression from scratch on 1,500 engineering firms to see whether a marketing claim survives basic causal scrutiny. Hand-rolled MLE, a counterfactual, and an honest answer.

title: Replicating Card & Krueger (1994): When the Minimum Wage Didn't Bite
summary: Card & Krueger's 1994 paper found that New Jersey's minimum-wage hike raised fast-food employment relative to Pennsylvania. Replicating their Table 4 from the raw survey data hits the coefficient exactly, until HC1 standard errors quietly erase its significance.

title: A/B Testing a Call to Action: Why Getting the Math Right Isn't Enough
summary: Statistical tests validate your calculations, not your decisions, and the way you interact with the test can break its validity.

title: The Dashboard That Lied By Telling the Truth
summary: A metric can be perfectly accurate and completely misleading at the same time. On the gap between measurement and understanding.
